purpose: Git ignore rules for the Synapse repo. Excludes runtime swarm data (every dashboard's progress files, initialization.json, logs.json, plus the entire dashboards/, tasks/, Archive/, history/, queue/ trees), build output (dist/), dependencies (node_modules/), macOS metadata (.DS_Store), user-local commands (_commands/user/), an entire `_parent/*` carve-out (and its claude-code settings), and saved conversations (conversations/*). Effectively encodes the boundary between source-controlled artifacts and per-machine runtime/user data.
gotchas: Lines 1-7 ignore SPECIFIC files inside dashboards/* (progress/*.json, initialization.json, logs.json), but lines 30-35 ignore the ENTIRE dashboards/, queue/, history/, Archive/, tasks/ trees with a wildcard. The wildcard at the bottom supersedes the granular rules — meaning even the parent dashboard directories are git-ignored, not just their JSON contents.

`conversations/*` is ignored but the `conversations/` directory itself is tracked (because of the wildcard, not a slash) — empty conversations directory will show as untracked unless a placeholder file exists.

`_parent/*` and `_parent/.claude/settings.local.json` look like leftover scaffolding from a previous repo layout where Synapse lived inside a parent project — the rule is preserved for safety but the directory may not exist in clean clones.
No `.env`, `.env.local`, or secrets glob — Synapse currently has no env files, but if any are added in the future, they will accidentally be tracked unless this file is updated.
`node_modules/` is ignored without a trailing `/**` — git treats both forms identically for directories, but linters may flag this.
No `.vscode/` or `.idea/` — IDE configs are not ignored; commit hygiene depends on individual contributors.
patterns: Layered ignore pattern: granular ignores at the top (specific files) plus broad wildcard ignores at the bottom (entire directories) — the wildcard wins, so the granular rules are de facto redundant in this repo.

Runtime-vs-source separation: every directory that holds runtime swarm artifacts (dashboards, tasks, Archive, history, queue) is excluded; everything else (electron/, src/, _commands/, agent/, documentation/) is tracked.
Section headers in comments (`# Runtime data`, `# Build output`, `# Dependencies`, `# OS`) document the ignore taxonomy.
conventions: Hash-prefixed comments (`# Runtime data`, etc.) group related rules.
